text: Code reviews are a vital practice in software development. When developers review each other's code, they can catch bugs and logic errors early, before the code reaches production. This saves significant time and effort compared to fixing problems later. Beyond finding mistakes, code reviews promote knowledge sharing within the team. Junior developers learn from more experienced colleagues, while everyone gains a broader understanding of the project. Over time, this process builds a stronger, more collaborative team that consistently produces higher-quality software. Good code reviews are not about criticism; they are about mutual support and continuous improvement.
